1.solution: Let t=log₂x (x>0). log₂(x⁴)=4t, (log₂x²)²=4t². Equation: x^(4t²+4t−12)=x^(−4). For x≠1: 4t²+4t−12=−4 → t²+t−2=0 → (t+2)(t−1)=0 → t=−2 (x=1/4) or t=1 (x=2). x=1 also works: 1^(−12)=1=1/1⁴. Integer solutions: {1,2}. Sum=3. Answer: D.

3.solution: log₂[log₃(x²+8x+24)]=1 → log₃(x²+8x+24)=2¹=2 → x²+8x+24=9 → x²+8x+15=0 → (x+3)(x+5)=0 → x=−3 or x=−5. Both give x²+8x+24=9>0 and log₃9=2>0 (valid). Possible values: 2.

5.solution: log₄(mn)=log₂(m+n) → (1/2)log₂(mn)=log₂(m+n) → √(mn)=m+n. Let t=√(m/n)>0: t+1/t=1. But t+1/t≥2 for all t>0 (AM-GM) — contradiction. No positive real m,n satisfy this equation. None of the given relationships A, B, C can hold. Answer: D.

6.solution: log₃x+log₃y≥4 → log₃(xy)≥4 → xy≥81. By AM-GM: x+y≥2√(xy)≥2√81=2×9=18. Equality when x=y=9: log₃9+log₃9=2+2=4✓. Minimum x+y=18.

8.solution: (P−8)^((log₂P)²−2log₂P)=(P−8)^15. Let t=log₂P. Case P=8: 0^3=0=0^15✓. Case P=9: 1^(any)=1=1^15✓. Case P−8≠0,1: t²−2t=15 → (t−5)(t+3)=0 → t=5→P=32 (integer✓,24^15=24^15✓); t=−3→P=1/8 (not integer). Integer solutions: {8,9,32}, count=3.

9.solution: Group: −14log_x5−14log_x4=−14log_x20 and 22log_x10+22log_x2=22log_x20. Sum=8log₂₀x+(−14+22)log_x20=8log₂₀x+8log_x20. Let u=log₂₀x>0 (x>1): f=8u+8/u≥2√(8·8)=16 by AM-GM, equality at u=1 (x=20). Minimum=16. Answer: B.

10.solution: LHS: log₃x+log₉x=log₃x+(log₃x)/2=(3/2)log₃x. RHS: log_{1/3}√12=log_{1/3}(12^(1/2))=(1/2)log_{1/3}12=−(1/2)log₃12. Equation: (3/2)log₃x=−(1/2)log₃12 → log₃x=−(1/3)log₃12=log₃(12^(−1/3)) → x=12^(−1/3). Answer: C.
